text: The gcd function implementation is incorrect because it causes infinite recursion and never reaches the base case when b != 0. The recursive call should swap the arguments to gcd(b, a % b) instead of gcd(a % b, b). This bug leads to maximum recursion depth exceeded errors in the tests.

data.label: The gcd function causes infinite recursion and fails the tests.
The recursive call in gcd should be gcd(b, a % b) not gcd(a % b, b).
Correct gcd algorithm uses gcd(b, a % b) recursion step.
